title: Manti Turkish Dumplings Yogurt

seo.title: Turkish Manti Recipe: Authentic Dumplings with Yogurt Sauce
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Turkish Manti, delicious tiny beef dumplings served with a creamy garlic yogurt sauce and rich spiced butter. Get our easy step-by-step recipe!
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Manti Recipe: Dumplings with Yogurt Sauce
h2Headings: How to Make Turkish Manti: Step-by-Step
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Turkish Manti dumplings served with garlic yogurt sauce and spiced butter, garnished with mint.
seo.imageCaption: Homemade Turkish Manti: tiny beef dumplings with a creamy garlic yogurt and rich spiced butter.

description: Turkish Manti are delicate tiny dumplings filled with spiced ground beef, a beloved staple of Turkish cuisine. Served generously with a creamy garlic yogurt sauce and drizzled with a rich, aromatic spiced butter, it's a dish that promises comfort and rich flavors. This recipe guides you through making these delightful pasta parcels from scratch for an authentic culinary experience.

instructions.title: Prepare the Manti Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour, egg, and salt. Gradually add water, mixing until a firm, elastic dough forms. Knead for 5-7 minutes until smooth. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for 15-20 minutes.

instructions.title: Make the Beef Filling
instructions.text: While the dough rests, mix the ground beef with grated onion, black pepper, and red pepper flakes in a bowl. Season with a pinch of salt. Ensure all ingredients are well combined.

instructions.title: Roll and Cut the Dough
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into two or three portions. On a lightly floured surface, roll out each portion very thinly, about 1-2mm thick, into a large rectangle. Using a knife or pizza cutter, cut the dough into small squares, approximately 1.5-2 cm (0.6-0.8 inches) per side.

instructions.title: Fill and Shape the Manti
instructions.text: Place a tiny amount of the beef filling (about 1/4 teaspoon) in the center of each dough square. Pinch the four corners of each square together in the middle, creating a small, open-topped parcel. Ensure the filling is sealed well inside. Repeat for all squares.

instructions.title: Cook the Manti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully drop the manti into the boiling water in batches, ensuring they do not stick together. Cook for 7-10 minutes, or until the manti float to the surface and the dough is tender. Drain well.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yogurt and Spiced Butter
instructions.text: For the yogurt sauce, whisk plain yogurt with minced garlic and a pinch of salt until smooth. For the spiced butter, melt butter in a small pan over medium heat. Add tomato paste (if using), dried mint, and red pepper flakes. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant and the butter turns a rich red color.

instructions.title: Serve the Manti
instructions.text: Arrange the cooked manti on serving plates. Generously spoon the garlic yogurt sauce over the dumplings. Drizzle the hot spiced butter over the yogurt. Garnish with fresh mint or a sprinkle of sumac, if desired, and serve immediately.

tips: For truly tiny manti, roll the dough as thin as possible and cut squares no larger than 2cm. This takes practice but is key to authentic taste.
If you don't have fresh garlic for the yogurt, a small pinch of garlic powder can be used, but fresh is always preferred for flavor.
To save time, you can prepare the manti dumplings in advance and freeze them on a floured tray before transferring to a bag. Cook from frozen, adding a few extra minutes to the boiling time.
article: Manti Turkish Dumplings Yogurt is a beloved Turkish Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
